# R1.04 · Server-assisted client-side caching

> Cache in the application's own memory (L1 ETS) and let the server push invalidations over RESP3, so repeated reads
> of the same key skip the round-trip. Grounded in the real RESP3 push subscription (`Connector` protocol:3 +
> `Coherence.broadcast` PUBLISH `ecc:{table}:coh`) and `EchoMQ.Script.new/2` — the precomputed-SHA1, EVALSHA-first
> script cache that mirrors the same idea. Served at /redis-patterns/caching/client-side-caching.

## Pages

- [Hub](/redis-patterns/caching/client-side-caching): the pattern and its three dives.
- [RESP3 client tracking](/redis-patterns/caching/client-side-caching/client-tracking): the server-assisted near cache — `Connector.start_link(protocol: 3, push_to: self())` subscribing to the table's coherence channel.
- [The invalidation push](/redis-patterns/caching/client-side-caching/invalidation-push): when a key changes, `Coherence.broadcast` PUBLISHes the 29-byte `id<>":"<>version` message and the `Ring` applies newer-wins.
- [The SHA1 script-cache parallel](/redis-patterns/caching/client-side-caching/script-cache): `EchoMQ.Script.new/2` precomputes the SHA1 so the connector runs EVALSHA-first with a NOSCRIPT fallback — the real `coherence_drop` Lua, a parallel, not an EchoMQ door.

## Up

- [R1 · Caching](/redis-patterns/caching) · [Course home](/redis-patterns)
